q.en: At Iwo Jima, what secret weapon did the U.S. Marines have?
q.zh: 在硫磺岛，美国海军陆战队拥有什么秘密武器？

options.en: Navajo radiomen using a code no one else could understand
options.zh: 使用无人能懂的纳瓦霍语代码的无线电兵

options.en: A new kind of gun according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一种新型枪（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The Introduction says the marines had 'a team of Navajo radiomen trained to transmit messages in a code no one else can understand.'
explain.zh: 引言中说陆战队员有一支“受过训练、用无人能懂的代码传送信息的纳瓦霍无线电兵队伍”。

q.en: Who got the idea of using the Navajo language as a military code?
q.zh: 谁想出了用纳瓦霍语作为军事密码的主意？

options.en: A Japanese commander according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一名日本指挥官（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Philip Johnston, who grew up on the Navajo reservation
options.zh: 在纳瓦霍保留区长大的 Philip Johnston

explain.en: The book says newspaper stories 'caught the attention of Philip Johnston,' who had lived on the Navajo reservation and spoke the language.
explain.zh: 书中说报纸上的报道“引起了 Philip Johnston 的注意”，他曾在纳瓦霍保留区长大的并会说这种语言。

q.zh: 在词汇表中，“code talkers（密码通讯兵）”是指……

options.en: Native American soldiers who used their native language as a code
options.zh: 用本族语言作为密码的原住民士兵

options.en: Code breakers according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 密码破译员（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'code talkers (n.)' as 'Native American soldiers who used their native language as a code during World Wars I and II.'
explain.zh: 词汇表把 code talkers 定义为“在两次世界大战中用本族语言作为密码的原住民士兵”。

q.en: Why was Navajo especially good for a secret military code?
q.zh: 为什么纳瓦霍语特别适合作为军事密码？

options.en: It was written down everywhere according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它到处都有文字记载（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: It was only spoken, with no written symbols, and few outsiders understood it
options.zh: 它只有口语、没有书写符号，且几乎没有被外人听懂

options.en: The enemy already knew it well
It was easy for enemies to learn

explain.en: The book calls Navajo 'a hidden language' that 'was only spoken and had no written symbols,' so few people understood it.
explain.zh: 书中称纳瓦霍语是“隐藏的语言”，“只有口语且没有书写符号”，因此很少有人懂。

q.en: In the Navajo code, the word 'shush' (bear) was used to stand for which letter?
q.zh: 在纳瓦霍密码中，单词“shush”（熊）代表哪个字母？

options.en: C according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The code dictionary shows: 'B | shush | bear.'
explain.zh: 密码字典中显示：“B | shush | bear（熊）”。

q.en: About how many Navajo code talkers served in the Pacific during WWII?
q.zh: 二战期间大约有多少名纳瓦霍密码通讯兵在太平洋战区服役？

options.en: Fewer than 10 according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 不到10名（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book reports: 'All in all, more than four hundred Navajo code talkers served brilliantly in the Pacific.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“总共有超过四百名纳瓦霍密码通讯兵在太平洋战区表现出色。”

q.en: During the Battle of Iwo Jima, how many messages did six code talkers send and receive without a single error?
q.zh: 在硫磺岛战役中，六名密码通讯兵发送和接收了多少条信息而毫无差错？

options.en: Fewer than 10 according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 不到10条（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states: 'six code talkers sent and received more than eight hundred messages in the heat of combat without a single error.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“六名密码通讯兵在激烈战斗中发送和接收了超过八百条信息，没有一处错误。”

q.en: The book explains the Caesar Cipher and Rail Fence Cipher to show...
q.zh: 书中解释凯撒密码和栅栏密码是为了说明……

options.en: Different methods of encoding, or hiding the meaning of, messages
options.zh: 隐藏信息含义的不同编码方法

options.en: How to write the Navajo language according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 如何书写纳瓦霍语（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: These examples illustrate 'cryptographic methods' where 'the message itself is not hidden, but its meaning is.'
explain.zh: 这些例子说明了“密码学方法”，即“信息本身不隐藏，但其含义被隐藏”。

q.en: The author most likely wrote this book to...
q.zh: 作者写这本书最可能的目的是……

options.en: Entertain with a fictional tale according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 用虚构故事娱乐读者（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Inform readers about the code talkers' important but long-secret contribution
options.zh: 向读者介绍密码通讯兵重要却长期保密的贡献

explain.en: The book tells the true history of the Navajo code and notes 'it is the only spoken military code that has never been broken.'
explain.zh: 这本书讲述了纳瓦霍密码的真实历史，并指出“它是唯一从未被破译的口语军事密码”。

q.en: When did the government secrecy around the code talker program finally end?
q.zh: 围绕密码通讯兵计划的政府保密何时最终解除？

options.en: 1945 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book states: 'Finally, in 1968, the secrecy around the code talker program ended.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“最终在1968年，围绕密码通讯兵计划的保密结束了。”

prompt.en: Write a short paragraph explaining in your own words how the Navajo code worked, using one example from the book (such as 'shush' for B or 'iron fish' for submarine).
prompt.zh: 用你自己的话写一小段，解释纳瓦霍密码是如何运作的，并使用书中的一个例子（如用“shush”代表 B，或“iron fish”代表潜艇）。

en: Explain that Navajo words stood for letters or military things.
Give one dictionary example from the book to show the idea.
zh: 说明纳瓦霍语单词代表字母或军事事物。
从书中举一个密码字典的例子来展示这个思路。
